purpose: Give the host critique step a concrete v2-versus-v3 comparison target before any accepted-state, export, publication, or golden mutation is considered.
candidate_change_summary: Panel C mobility-edge label was demoted to lower visual hierarchy and moved away from the E_C caliper.
Panel C shallow label received a white backplate and clearer separation from the arrow field.
The deep escape curve endpoint was pulled slightly down and left so the arrow field has more breathing room.
No panel roles, required labels, symbol names, semantic colors, or mechanism claims were intentionally changed.
deterministic_evidence: fig-agent status reports render_state=FRESH and critique_state=MISSING.
fig-agent drive reports action=run_critique, required_actor=host_llm, and stop_boundary=host_llm_critique_required.
human_review_questions: Does v3 materially improve the Panel C mobility-edge/shallow readability problem compared with accepted v2?
Does v3 preserve the restrained manuscript overview style rather than becoming a new visual language?
Are there any remaining label-target, arrow-overlap, or semantic-readability defects that should return to source-level TikZ repair?
Is the candidate improvement strong enough to justify a separate acceptance/export/golden decision later?
agent_recommendation: Defer protected state mutation and run host vision critique against this packet; v3 is a plausible readability improvement, but the deterministic detectors only prove absence of configured proximity/text-boundary candidates, not final visual quality.

choices.label: Run host critique before deciding

follow_up.after_decision: rerun fig-agent status and queue for fig1-v3-candidate

choices.risk: Candidate remains blocked, but no protected state is mutated before visual review.

choices.label: Iterate source-level TikZ repair

follow_up.after_decision: open a bounded source repair task tied to specific host critique findings

choices.risk: Further source changes can improve readability but must stay inside the existing semantic contract.

choices.label: Keep v2 as the benchmark

follow_up.after_decision: leave v3 as an unaccepted candidate lane and continue other queue work

choices.risk: Panel C readability concern is not promoted into the accepted fixture.

choices.label: Prepare later release decision

follow_up.after_decision: only after fresh critique and human approval, prepare a separate acceptance/export/golden packet

choices.risk: Premature use would conflate visual comparison with protected release authority.

disallowed_actions: do not treat this packet as human acceptance
do not set accepted: true from this packet
do not copy v3 source over the accepted v2 fixture from this packet
do not export v3 as a golden artifact from this packet
do not hand-edit generated exports or SVG artifacts from this packet
do not attest or publish v3 from this packet

follow_up.implementation_slice: Run host vision critique, then decide whether to iterate v3, keep v2, or prepare a separate protected release decision packet.
